release.readiness: FULLY ANALYSIS-READY — ALL INSTRUCTOR-APPROVED CLEANING RULES APPLIED

q1.question: Which projects and material categories have the highest cost overruns in CAD and as a percentage of budget?
q1.calculation_method: Aggregate Budget_Value_CAD, PO_Value_CAD, and Cost_Variance_CAD by project or material category. Net Cost Overrun (%) = SUM(Cost_Variance_CAD) ÷ SUM(Budget_Value_CAD). Rank descending by net Cost_Variance_CAD. Gross positive exposure is reported separately as the sum of positive row-level variances.
q1.excluded_records: None. Delivery status does not affect cost analysis.
q1.positive_negative_rule: Positive variance means over budget; negative variance means under budget.

q2.question: Which suppliers are consistently pricing above budget — and by how much?
q2.definition_of_consistently: A supplier is consistently above budget when more than 50% of its purchase orders have positive Cost_Variance_CAD and its total Cost_Variance_CAD is positive.
q2.ranking_rule: Qualifying suppliers are ranked by Average Variance (CAD) descending; percentage of orders above budget is the tie-breaker.
q2.calculation_method: Average Variance = AVERAGE(Cost_Variance_CAD); Percentage of Orders Above Budget = COUNT(Cost_Variance_CAD > 0) ÷ Purchase Order Count.
q2.excluded_records: None. Delivery status does not affect supplier pricing.

q3.question: Does the procurement method used (e.g. direct award vs. open tender) affect the level of cost variance?
q3.calculation_method: Descriptive statistics are calculated from Cost_Variance_CAD by Procurement_Method. Because order sizes differ, the inferential test uses row-level Variance_% to compare methods on a normalized basis.
q3.excluded_records: No records were excluded. Invitational procurement has zero records and therefore cannot be statistically compared.
q3.interpretation: Procurement method does not appear to materially influence cost variance: the normalized ANOVA p-value is above 0.05 and eta squared is very small. This is an observational association, not a causal test.

q4.question: How is supplier delivery performance across the portfolio — what are average lead times and overall on-time delivery rates by category?
q4.calculation_method: Filter Delivery_Status = Delivered. Average Lead Time = AVERAGE(Lead_Time_Days); Median Lead Time = MEDIAN(Lead_Time_Days); On-Time Delivery Rate = COUNT(On_Time_Delivery = Yes) ÷ Delivered Orders.
q4.included_records: 204 Delivered orders, including the two instructor-approved 45-day Civil Works median imputations.
q4.excluded_records: 63 Pending, 65 Cancelled, and 68 Partially Delivered orders.
q4.ranking_rule: Rank by On-Time Delivery Rate descending, then Average Lead Time ascending, then name alphabetically.

lead_time_sensitivity.interpretation: The approved 45-day imputations change the Delivered-only portfolio average by less than 0.01 day and do not change the median, category ranking, supplier ranking, on-time rate, or recommendation.

q5.question: Based on the data, what are the top 3 recommendations ConstructEdge should act on in 2026?

recommendations.recommendation: Launch targeted commercial reviews with the suppliers that meet the consistently-above-budget rule.
recommendations.supporting_evidence: 3 suppliers qualify: Houle Electric, PCL Mechanical, Levitt-Safety Calgary. Their combined net positive variance is $2,022,856.51.
recommendations.expected_business_benefit: Reduces recurring supplier-level price leakage while preserving relationships with suppliers that do not show a consistent pattern.
recommendations.estimated_impact: A 50% reduction in their current net positive variance equals approximately $1,011,428.26.

recommendations.recommendation: Add pre-award variance thresholds and monthly exception reviews for the three projects with positive net cost variance.
recommendations.supporting_evidence: 3 projects are net over budget: Quarry Park Mixed-Use Development, Calgary SE Ring Road Extension, Deerfoot Trail Widening - North. Combined net overrun is $443,408.60.
recommendations.expected_business_benefit: Concentrates management attention where net overruns exist; the method analysis does not support a portfolio-wide sourcing-method change.
recommendations.estimated_impact: A 50% reduction in these project overruns equals approximately $221,704.30.

recommendations.recommendation: Create supplier delivery scorecards and corrective-action plans for material categories and suppliers below the Delivered-only portfolio benchmark.
recommendations.supporting_evidence: The Delivered-only on-time rate is 74.02%. 2 material categories are below that benchmark.
recommendations.expected_business_benefit: Improves schedule reliability and makes late-delivery performance visible during supplier reviews.
recommendations.estimated_impact: Bringing below-benchmark categories up to the portfolio rate would produce approximately 7 additional on-time deliveries across the current 204 Delivered orders.

q5.impact_note: Financial scenarios are directional and not additive because supplier and project variances can refer to the same purchase orders.
